name: Iolaus
description: Iolaus was a Theban hero, nephew and charioteer of Heracles. He was the son of Iphicles, Heracles' half-brother, and Automedusa. Iolaus is best known for his unwavering loyalty and assistance to Heracles throughout the hero's Twelve Labors and other adventures. He played a crucial role in defeating the Lernaean Hydra, cauterizing the Hydra's necks after Heracles severed them, preventing them from regenerating. This act was instrumental in Heracles' success. Beyond the Hydra, Iolaus accompanied Heracles on many other expeditions, including battles and quests for vengeance. He was a skilled warrior in his own right, often proving to be a valuable ally. After Heracles' death, Iolaus protected Heracles' children from Eurystheus, eventually killing Eurystheus in battle. He later led a group of Heraclids to Sardinia, where he established a colony. According to some accounts, Iolaus was granted a temporary rejuvenation by Hebe, allowing him to defeat Eurystheus. Iolaus represents unwavering loyalty, resourcefulness, and the importance of companionship in achieving heroic deeds. His actions ensured Heracles' success and protected his lineage.
subtitle: Loyal Companion of Heracles
achievements: Assisted Heracles in defeating the Lernaean Hydra
Protected Heracles' children after his death
Participated in numerous adventures with Heracles

keyMyths.title: Defeating the Lernaean Hydra
keyMyths.description: Iolaus cauterized the Hydra's severed necks with fire, preventing them from regenerating, thus allowing Heracles to kill the monster. His quick thinking and resourcefulness were vital to Heracles' success in this labor.

keyMyths.title: Protecting the Heraclids
keyMyths.description: After Heracles' death, Iolaus shielded his children from the persecution of Eurystheus. He demonstrated courage and loyalty by ensuring their safety and ultimately avenging their father.

keyMyths.title: Killing Eurystheus
keyMyths.description: Iolaus pursued and killed Eurystheus, ending his reign of terror and fulfilling the prophecy that Heracles' descendants would eventually overthrow him. This act brought peace and justice to the land.

sources.author: Apollodorus

sources.text: A comprehensive compendium of Greek myths and legends.
